w:r.w:t: Xtream UI R22 Backend Administration API Manual
Enterprise Billing Integration & Lifecycle Provisioning Specification
This reference manual defines the complete backend API specification required to link an independent billing engine or storefront platform to an
Xtream UI R22 / XUI core panel

. This version incorporates commercial provisioning standards, secure credential parameters, and operational validation controls to ensure automated subscription processing, billing renewals, and failure handling maps perfectly to production requirements.

All automated transactions must entirely bypass public streaming or player ports (such as standard port 80 or 8080). Direct your internal gateway scripts exclusively to the panel's central CMS manager administrative core port.
Every administrative payload must securely pass root or executive reseller verification flags inside the query arguments string:
The administrative dashboard gateway user credential.
The administrative dashboard gateway authentication password.

Xtream UI administrative CMS ports (commonly port 25500) must be firewalled via iptables or ufw on your core media server. Explicitly whitelist only the public IP address of your dedicated billing server platform to prevent unauthenticated access or remote execution risks.

Executed automatically by your billing engine's webhook handlers once a client invoice status transitions to Paid. This provisions a unique line in the database, applies simultaneous stream boundaries, and appends a fixed expiration date.
Strip client's email prefix and append a random 4-char suffix (e.g., john_a8k2).
Generate a strict 10-character random alphanumeric string at checkout.
Set dynamically based on the specific product package tier purchased.
Calculate mathematically via time() stamp plus inventory plan duration.
URL-encoded integer list of Bouquet IDs.
Extracted dynamically from your storefront package database mapping.
Modify or Renew an Active Line Profile (sub=edit)
Invoked automatically for plan renewals, extensions, or product tier alterations. Do not generate a duplicate profile on renewal cycles; instead, update the existing database entry.

Passing the bouquet parameter array completely flushes and replaces previous channel rows in the database. If your system allows distinct modular content add-ons or upsell packages, your middleware application must compute the combined mathematical array of existing and new Bouquet IDs before transmitting the edit payload.
Triggers automatically when an invoice grace period lapses. This immediately terminates active client network streams and drops active TCP authentication challenges at the gateway.
Issue an identical payload query string structure to the administrative URL but adjust the parameter flag to

Used to establish package mapping relations inside your central vendor control board. Your script calls this endpoint to extract active Bouquet Names and database IDs, eliminating manual data sync errors.
When designing code loops or logging telemetry data inside your provisioning engine, map panel exceptions directly to remediation paths using this matrix:
Admin/executive credentials failed validation checks or API is disabled.
Audit admin panel variables. Ensure API access is enabled under Settings > General Settings.
The generated credential identity maps to an existing user line row.
Adjust your provisioning generator to append a unique, long randomized alphanumeric suffix configuration.
Target profile username missing from table references during edit/suspension.
Log transaction exception. Confirm record state and verify the identity field was not dropped from the database.
Network routing layer block, firewall rejection, or dead panel process.
Verify target server firewall accepts your billing engine IP. SSH into the panel host and execute: systemctl status xtreamcodes.
